provisions.title: Citation and commencement
provisions.content: These Regulations may be cited as the Merchant Shipping (Fees) Regulations, 2005 and shall come into operation on publication date.

provisions.title: Interpretation
provisions.content: In these Regulations, unless the Context otherwise requires:— “ Act ” means the Merchant Shipping Act, 2003; “ Authority ” means the Surface and Marine Transport Regulatory Authority; “ BCH Code ” means the Code for the Construction and Equipment of Ships Carrying Dangerous Chemicals in Bulk; “ crew agreement ” has the same meaning assigned to it by section 111 of the Act ; “ deadweight ” means the total mass of cargo, fuel, fresh water, that a ship can carry when she is floating in salt water with her summer load line at the water surface; “ deck officer ” means an officer whose duties are connected with the deck department; “ DWT ” means Deadweight; “ dry dock ” means an excavated dock fitted with watertight entrance, from which water can be pumped to allow work to be done on or under the water portion of a docked ship ; “ IBC Code Certificate ” means the International Bulk Carrier Code for the construction and equipment of ships carrying dangerous chemicals in bulk; “ IGC Code Certificate ” means international code for construction and equipment of ships carrying liquefied gases in bulk; “ INLS Certificate ” means an international pollution prevention certificate for the carriage of Noxious Liquid Substances in bulk issued in conformity with Annex II to MARPOL 73/78 ; “ IOPP Certificate ” means International Oil Pollution Prevention Certificate issued in conformity with annex I to MARPOL 73/78 ; “ ISPS Code ” means International Ship & Port Facility Security code; “ MARPOL 73/78 ” means the international convention for the prevention of Pollution from ships, 1973, as modified by the Protocol of 1978 relating thereto; “ mortgage ” means an instrument of security of the kind referred to in section 88 of the Act ; “ noxious liquid substance ” means any substance referred in appendix II to annex II of MARPOL 73/78 ; “ proper officer ” means any officer appointed under the Act to perform a certain duty or function when engaged on the performance of that duty or function and includes a consular officer; “ rating ” means seaman other than officer; “ ship ” means a floating vessel , which is self propelled and capable of carrying passengers or cargo; “ GT ” means gross tonnage; “ small ship ” means a ship of less than 24 meters in length or less than 50 gross registered tonnage; “ survey ” means a visit on board a ship to make a detailed examination, inspection or investigation to determine the validity of the relevant certificates and other documents, and the condition of the ship ’s hull, equipment, machinery and its crew; “ tonnage convention ” means the International Convention on Tonnage Measurement of Ships, 1969; “ tons ” means gross tons, and the tonnage of a ship having alternative gross tonnages shall be taken to be the larger of those tonnages; and “ vessel ” includes any ship , boat, sailing vessel, or other vessel of any description used in navigation. Part II – Services and issuance of documents

provisions.title: Issue of Certificate
provisions.content: The following service and officers shall be charged for issuance of certificates under these Regulations as specified under the First Schedule: (1) rating forming part of a navigational watch; (2) ratings forming part of an engineering watch; (3) deck officer ; (4) engineer officer; (5) mandatory; (6) endorsement; (7) able bodied seamen and Oiler; (8) compass adjuster; and (9) extension of validity of Certificate of Competency and Qualifications.

provisions.title: Manning
provisions.content: The following documents and services shall be charged for manning as specified under the Second Schedule to these Regulations: (1) crew Article; (2) seamen record book; and (3) dispensation and evaluation of foreign certificates.

provisions.title: Registration and licensing
provisions.content: (1) The fees payable for the following services and registrations shall be as specified under the Third Schedule to these Regulations: (a) initial registration; (b) change of name of a ship ; (c) change of ownership of a ship ; (d) bill of Sale; (e) discharge of mortgage ; (f) registration a new as a result of alternation to ship ; (g) registration of alteration to ship , except registry a new as a result of such alteration; (h) provisional registration; (i) replacement of Certificate of Registry; (j) registration of deed of mortgage ; (k) licensing of unregistered vessels (vessels under 50 GT ); (l) licensing of unregistered foreign vessels; and (m) witnessing of a declaration before the Registrar of Ships. (2) There shall be charges to be known as annual ship fee paid during annual survey as specified under the Third Schedule to these Regulations.

provisions.title: Tonnage measurement/ survey
provisions.content: (1) The following shall be charged for Tonnage measurements/ surveys of a ship of registered length as specified under the Fourth Schedule to these Regulations: (a) sailing vessel and vessels propelled by oars or paddles (vessels under 50 GT ); (b) mechanically propelled vessels and vessels tilled with auxiliary engines, (vessels under 50 GT ) (c) less than 24 meters; (d) 24 meters and over but not exceeding 36 meters; (e) more than 36 meters, but not exceeding 55 meters; (f) more than 55 meters, but not exceeding 68 meters; and (g) more than 68 meters. (2) In the case of a ship that has been measured in a foreign port by arrangement with the government of the country in which that port is situated or with some other responsible body or person, for the purpose of provisional registration or otherwise shall be charged a fee as specified under the Fourth Schedule to these Regulations (3) There shall be no charge payable in respect of a foreign ship registered at a port in the United Republic, unless an alteration in the form or capacity of the ship has been made since4he first measurement.

provisions.title: Survey of passenger ship
provisions.content: The following passenger ships shall be charged for the surveys of safety certificates as specified under the Fourth Schedule to these Regulations: (a) sailing vessel and vessels propelled by oars or paddles (vessels under 50 GT ); (b) mechanically propelled vessels and vessels tilled with auxiliary engines, (vessels under 50 GT ) (c) 50 tons to 100 tons ; (d) over 100 tons to 300 tons ; (e) oven 300 tons to 600 tons ; and (f) over 600 tons .

provisions.title: Survey of a Cargo Ship Safety Certificate
provisions.content: There shall be charges for the following ships which shall be payable for the survey of a Cargos Ship Safety Certificate with or without an exemption certificate as specified under the Fourth Schedule to these Regulations: (a) 500 tons or over, but under 1000 tons ; (b) 1000 tons or over, but under 3000 tons ; (c) 3000 tops or over, but under 6000 tons ; (d) 6000 tons or over, but under 8000 tons ; (e) 8000 tons or over, but under 9000 tons ; (f) 9000 tons or over, but under 20,000 tons ; (g) 20,000 tons or over, but under 50,000 tons ; and (h) 50,000 tons or over.

provisions.title: Survey of a ship for a Local Safety Certificate
provisions.content: The following ships shall be charged for the survey of Local Safety Certificate with or without a Local Safety Exemption Certificate, and for the issuance of a record of safety equipment as specified under the Fourth Schedule to these Regulations: (1) sailing vessel and vessels propelled by oars or paddles (vessels under 50 GT ); (2) mechanically propelled vessels and vessels tilled with auxiliary engines, (vessels under 50 GT ) (3) ships 50 tons to 100 tons ; (4) ships over 100 tons to 500 tons ; (5) ships over 500 tons to 1600 tons ; (6) ships over 1600 tons to 3000 tons ; and (7) ships over 3000 tons .

provisions.title: Survey of a ship for IGC Code Certificate
provisions.content: The following services shall be charged for survey of a ship for an IGC Code certificate as specified under Part III: (a) intermediate, annual or additional survey ; (b) where a surveyor carries out a survey or inspection to determine whether certain equipment or documents comply with the prescribed specifications; and (c) if a certificate of inspection is required.

provisions.title: Survey of a ship for a Load Line Certificate
provisions.content: The following shall be charged for the survey of a Load Line Certificate with or without a load line exemption certificate as specified under the Fifth Schedule to these Regulations: (a) 50 tons to 150 tons ; (b) over 150 tons to 300 tons ; (c) over 300 tons to 500 tons ; (d) over 500 tons to 1000 tons ; (e) over 1000 tons to 1500 tons ; (f) over 1500 tons to 2000 tons ; (g) over 2000 tons to 4000 tons ; (h) over 4000 tons to 6000 tons ; (i) over 6000 tons to 8000 tons ; (j) over 8000 tons to 10000 tons ; and (k) over 10000 tons .

provisions.title: General charges
provisions.content: The following documents and services shall be charged as specified under Sixth Schedule to these Regulations: (1) Safe Manning Certificate; (2) Safety Management Certificate; (3) Document of Compliance; (4) Survey for Cargo Ship Safety Construction Certificate; (5) Survey for Cargo Ship Safety Equipment Certificate; (6) Survey for Cargo Ship Safety Radio Certificate; (7) Inspection of life-saving appliances arid life-raft service stations; (8) Inspection of fire appliances and service stations; (9) Inspection of crew accommodation; (10) Dangerous cargoes; (11) Grain cargoes; (12) Timber deck cargoes; (13) Partial inspections; (14) Certificate of Competency for Engineers; (15) Certificate of competency for deck officer ; (16) Certificate of Competency for small vessel ; (17) Certificates of qualification; (18) Licensing of compass adjuster; (19) Dispensations and evaluation of foreign certificate of competency; (20) Survey of ship for IOPP certificate; (21) Survey of ship for INLS certificate; (22) Survey of ship for IBC Code Certificate ; (23) Survey of ship for BCH Code Certificate; (24) Survey of ship for IGC Code Certificate ; (25) Survey of offshore installation for pollution safety certificate; (26) Certificate of insurance for marine pollution Liability; (27) Survey or inspection of equipment and documents; (28) Survey or inspection under Annex II to MARPOL 1973/78; (29) Provisional assessment of noxious liquid substances; (30) Transshipment of oil; (31) Detention charge; (32) Record book and official logbook; (33) Engagement and discharge of seamen; (34) Extra and special attendance; (35) Surveys outside the United Republic; (36) Inland attendance and Extra Ordinary attendance; (37) Consultancy services; (38) Exemption Certificate; (39) Expenses for Receiver of Wrecks; (40) Travel and subsistence expenses; (41) Copies of documents; (42) Delegated functions; (43) Survey of ship in dry dock ; (44) Certificates for ISPS Code compliance; (45) Extension of Validity of Certificate; (46) Penalty; (47) Payment of fees Part III – Marine pollution charges

provisions.title: Survey of ship for IOPP certificate
provisions.content: The following charges shall be payable for the survey of a ship for an IOPP certificate: (a) initial or renewal survey where the period of validity of the certificate is five years: (iii) fishing vessel — US$ 30; (iii) ship (other than an Oil Tanker) over 400 tons — US$ 255; (iii) oil tanker over 150 tons , up to 4 000 DWT — US$ 255; (iv) oil tanker over 4 000 DWT — US$ 400. (b) annual survey — US$ 70; and (c) intermediate or additional survey US$ 120.

provisions.title: Survey of ship for INLS Certificate
provisions.content: The following charges shall be payable for the survey of a ship for an INLS certificate: (a) initial or renewal survey where the period of validity of the certificate is five years: (i) chemical tanker (all ship types) — US$ 514 (ii) oil tanker capable of carrying specified noxious liquid substances in bulk in designated cargo tanks, per tank US$35. (b) annual survey : (i) chemical tanker- US$ 500; (ii) oil tanker capable of carrying specified noxious liquid substances in bulk in designated cargo tanks, tank — US$ 15; (c) intermediate or additional survey : (i) chemical tanker- US$ 255; (ii) oil tanker capable of carrying specified noxious liquid substances in bulk in designated cargo tanks, per tank — US$ 20.

provisions.title: Survey of ship for IBC Code Certificate
provisions.content: The following charges shall be payable for the survey of a ship for an IBC Code certificate: (a) initial or renewal survey where the period of validity of the certificate is Five years — US$ 600; and (b) intermediate, annual or additional survey US$ 30 per surveyor-hour or part thereof.

provisions.title: Survey of ship for BCH Code Certificate
provisions.content: The following charges shall be payable for the survey of a ship for a BCH Code certificate: (a) initial or renewal survey where the period of validity of the certificate is five years — US$ 600; and (b) intermediate, annual or additional survey - US$ 30 per surveyor-hour or part thereof.

provisions.title: Survey of Ships for IGC-Code
provisions.content: The following charges shall be payable for the survey of a ship for a IGC Code certificate: (a) initial or renewal, survey where the period of validity of the certificate is five years — US$ 600; (b) intermediate, annual or additional survey - US$ 30 per surveyor-hour or part thereof.

provisions.title: Survey of offshore installation for Pollution Safety Certificate
provisions.content: A charge of US$ 700 and the travel and subsistence expenses of the surveyor concerned shall be payable in respect of the survey of an offshore installation for a Pollution Safety Certificate.

provisions.title: Certificates of Insurance for marine pollution liability
provisions.content: A charge of US$ 145 shall be payable for the issuance of a Certificate of Insurance or other financial security referred to in the Civil Liability for Oil Pollution Damage, 1969.

provisions.title: Survey or inspection of equipment and documents
provisions.content: (1) Where a surveyor carries out a survey or inspection to determine whether certain equipment or documents comply with the prescribed specifications, a charge of US$ 30 per surveyor-hour or part thereof shall be payable. (2) If a certificate of inspection is required, an additional charge of US$ 30 shall be payable. (3) A charge of US$ 10 shall be payable for the issuance of a certificate of approval based on the approval of another competent authority.

provisions.title: Survey or inspection under Annex II to MARPOL 1973/78
provisions.content: (1) Where a surveyor carries out a survey or inspection to determine whether the provisions of Annex II to MARPOL 1973/78 have been complied with, a charge of US$ 30 per cargo tank surveyed or inspected is payable. (2) If a certificate of inspection is required, an additional charge of US$ 30 shall be payable.

provisions.title: Provisional assessment of Noxious Liquid Substances
provisions.content: A charge of US$ 130 shall be payable for each Noxious Liquid Substance provisionally assessed under regulation 3(4) of Annex II to MAR POL 1973/78.

provisions.title: Transshipment of oil
provisions.content: A charge of US$ 145 per day, or US$ 10 per hour if it is not a full day, and the officer’s travel and subsistence of expense shall be payable for the attendance of a representing officer.

provisions.title: Detention charge
provisions.content: Where a ship is detained under the Act , a charge of US$ 150 shall be payable. Part IV – Miscellaneous charges

provisions.title: Seaman’s record book and official logbook
provisions.content: (1) A charge of US$ 10 shall be payable for a seaman’s record book, whether the application is made at a place where there is a proper officer or at a place where there is no proper officer or to replace a lost, mutilated or damaged or full record book, unless the record book has been lost, mutilated or damaged due to shipwreck or fire on board ship . (2) A charge of US$ 25 shall be payable for an official logbook.

provisions.title: Engagement and discharge of seaman
provisions.content: (1) Subject to sub-regulations (2) and (3) , where a seaman is engaged or discharged before a proper officer under section 113(1) or (4) of the Act, a charge of US$ 2 shall be payable by the master or owner of the ship from which the seaman is engaged or discharged. (2) Where the engagement or discharge of a seaman is effected on board ship at a port in the United Republic, the charge payable in terms of subparagraph (1) is subject to an additional charge of US$ 20 for each visit that the proper officer is required to make to the ship in order to complete such discharge. (3) Where the engagement or discharge of a seaman is effected on board ship at a port outside the United Republic at the request of the master or owner of the ship , the proper officer ’s travel and subsistence expenses shall be payable in addition to the applicable charges on normal working hours and US$ 30 outside the Authority ’s normal working hours.

provisions.title: Extra and special attendance
provisions.content: A charge of US$ 30 per hour or part thereof and the surveyor or proper officer travel and subsistence expenses shall be payable for the attendance of a surveyor or proper officer on a Saturday, a Sunday, a public holiday or at any other time outside the Authority ’s normal working hours.

provisions.title: Surveys outside the United Republic
provisions.content: Where the attendance of a surveyor or proper officer is required outside the United Republic, the surveyor’s or proper officer ’s travel and subsistence expenses shall be payable in addition to any applicable charge.

provisions.title: Inland attendances and extraordinary attendance
provisions.content: Where the attendance of a proper officer is required at any place in the United Republic outside the times determined by the Authority , the proper officer ’s travel and subsistence expenses shall be payable in addition to any applicable charge.

provisions.title: Consultancy services
provisions.content: (1) Where a person requests a consultancy service, the charge prescribed for that service shall be payable. (2) If the service requested is not fully specified in this determination, a charge of US$ 30 per surveyor-hour or part thereof shall be payable. (3) Where an officer is requested to interpret any requirement applicable to a particular class or category of ship , a charge of US$ 30 per surveyor-hour or part thereof shall be payable.

provisions.title: Exemption Certificate
provisions.content: A charge of US$ 100 shall be payable when an Exemption Certificate is granted to a ship under and in accordance with the provisions of the Act .

provisions.title: Expenses of the Receiver of Wrecks
provisions.content: The Receiver of Wrecks shall be reimbursed the expenses incurred by him in performing his duties

provisions.title: Travel and subsistence expenses
provisions.content: Unless otherwise stated, travel and subsistence expenses shall be calculated in accordance with the tariffs approved by the Authority .

provisions.title: Copies of documents
provisions.content: Where a person requests a copy of a document belonging to the Authority for commercial purposes, a charge of US$ 20 shall be payable.

provisions.title: Delegated functions
provisions.content: The Authority may charge fees for functions delegated.

provisions.title: Survey of ship in dry dock
provisions.content: There shall be a charge of US$ 100 which shall be payable for survey of ship in dry dock , for vessels above 50 GT .

provisions.title: Certificates for ISPS Code compliance
provisions.content: There shall be a charge of US$ 25 for issuance of ISPS Certificate.

provisions.title: Extension of validity of certificate
provisions.content: There shall be a fee charged at the rate of 25% of the annual fee paid for the extension of validity of a certificate.

provisions.title: Penalty
provisions.content: (1) Interest at a rate of 20% per annum, compounded monthly, shall be payable in respect of the outstanding balance of any charge after the due date. (2) Offences committed to obstruct a proper officer or surveyor to perform his duties, shall be liable as under Part XXII of the Act .

provisions.title: Payment of fees
provisions.content: All fees shall be: (a) paid to the Registrar of Ships; (b) paid within time stated in the invoice; (c) paid in the equivalent in Tanzanian Shillings of the United States Dollars; and (d) none refundable. First Schedule (Made under regulation 3) Issue of Certificates (1) rating forming part of a navigational watch US$ 5; (2) rating forming part of an engineering watch US$ 5; (3) deck officer US$ 25; (4) engineer officer US$ 25; (5) mandatory US$ 5; (6) endorsement US$ 5; (7) able bodied seamen and oiler US $ 5; (8) compass adjuster US$ 20; and (9) extension of validity of Certificate of Competency and Qualifications US$ 10. Second Schedule (Made under regulation 4) Manning (a) Crew Article US$ 20 (b) Seamen record books US$ 10 (c) Dispensation and evaluation of foreign certificate US$ 100 per year. Third Schedule (Made under regulation 5) A. Registration (1) initial Registration US$ 500 irrespective of the size of the vessel; (2) change of name of a ship US$ 50; (3) charge of ownership of a ship US$ 150; (4) Bill of Sale US$ 100; (5) discharge of mortgage US$ 250; (6) registration anew as a result of alteration to ship US$ 500; (7) registration of alteration to ship except registry a new as a result of such alteration US$ 250; (8) provisional registration US$ 200; (9) replacement of Certificate Registry US$ 150; (10) registration of mortgage; US$ 120; (11) licensing of Unregistered vessels (Vessels of 50 GT and above) US$ 500; (12) witnessing of a declaration before the Registrar of Ships US$ 10. B. Annual ship fee US$ 20 for initial 1,000 Tons; for every lOO Tons or thereof over 1,000 tons, US$ 50 cents C. Unregistered Vessels (vessels under 50 GT) 1. License fees (1) sailing vessels and vessels propelled by oars or paddles: (a) up to and including 4 meters in length US$ 5; and (b) for every meter or part thereof over 4 meters, Us$ 1. (2) Mechanically propelled vessels and vessels tilled with auxiliary engines: (a) up to and including 4 meters in length US$ 10; and (b) for every meters or part thereof over 4 meters, US$ 1. 2. Transfer of license Fees for change of a licensed vessel's name, change of port of licensing, transfer of license, transfer of mortgage, bill of sale, mortgage and discharge of mortgage, all licensed vessels under 50 GT, for each service US$ 20. Fourth Schedule (Made under regulation 6) 1. Surveys of ships for tonnage measurement (a) less than 24 meters US$ 65; (b) 24 meters and over, but not exceeding 36 meters, US$ 130; (c) more than 36 meters, but not exceeding 55 meters, US$ 300; (d) more than 55 meters, but not exceeding 68 meters US $ 600; (e) more than 68 meters US$ 750; (f) in the case of a ship that has been measured in a foreign port by arrangement with the government of the country in which that port is situated or with some other responsible body or person, for the purpose of provisional registration or otherwise, a charge of US$ 150 shall payable for checking the tonnage computation. 2. Survey of passenger ship for safety certificate (a) up to 50 tons US$ 200 (b) over 50 tons to 100 tons US$ 250; (c) over 100 tons to 300 tons US$ 300; (d) over 300 tons to 600 tons US$ 400; and (e) over 600 tons US$ 500 plus US$ 10 for each 100 tons of part thereof above 600 tons; 3. Survey for Cargo Ship Safety Certificate Item Tonnage of ship Charge (1) (2) (3) Survey for first issue of certificate Survey for renewal of certificate Intermediate or annual survey during period of validity of certificate 1. 500 tons or over, but under 1000 US$ 975 US$ 875: US$ 775 2. 1000 tons or over, but under 3000 tons US$ 1200 US$ 1l00 US$ 1000 3. 3000 tons or over, but under 6000 tons US$ 1500 US$ 1400 US$ 1300 4. 6,000 tons or over but under 8000 US$ 2500 US$ 2400 US$ 2300 5. 8000 tons or over, but under 9000 tons US$ 2600 US$ 2500 US$ 2400 6. 9000 tons or over but under 20,000 tons US$ 2700 US$ 2600 US$ 2500 7. 20000 tons or over, but under 50000 US$ 2900 US$ 2800US US$ 2700 8. 50000 tons or over US$ 3500 $3400 US$ 3300 4. Survey of a ship for Local Safety Certificate Item Description of ship charges First issue Annual/Renewal 1. Sailing vessels and vessels propelled by oars or paddles, up to and including 4 meters in length. 5 5 For every meter or part thereof over 4 meters Us$ 1 2. Mechanically propelled vessels and vessels tilled with auxiliary engines, Up to and including 4 meters in length 10US$ US$ 10 For every meter or part thereof over 4 meters US$ 1. (Vessels under 50 GT) 3. Ships 50 tons to 100 tons 375USS US$ 300 4. Ships over 100 tons to 500 tons 550USS US$ 500 5. Ships over 500 tons to 1600 tons 750USS US$ 700 6. Ships over 1600 tons to 3000 tons 1000USS US$ 950 7. Ships, over 3000 tons 1200 US$ 1000 5. (1) Survey of ship for IGC (International Gas Carrier) code certificate US$ 700; (2) Intermediate or annual or additional survey US$ 400; (3) Survey or inspection to determine whether certain equipment or documents comply with the prescribed specifications, US$ 400; (4) If a certificate of inspection is required, US$ 40. Fifth Schedule (Made under regulation 11) Load Line Survey: Item Description of ship Survey for 1 st issue or renewal of certificate Intermediate or annual survey 1, Under 50 tons US $ 25 US$ 10 2. 50 - 150 tons US$ 105 US$ 35 3. 150 - 300 tons US$ 150 US$ 50 4. 300 - 500 tons US$ 180 US$ 60 5. 500 - 1000 tons US$ 225 US$ 75 6. 1000 - 1500 tons US$270 US$ 90 7. 1500 - 2000 tons US$ 315 US$ 105 8. 2000 - 4000 tons US$ 360 US$ 120 9. 4000 - 6000 tons US$ 405 US$ 135 10. 6000 - 8000 tons US$ 450 US$ 150 11. 8000 - 10,000 tons US$ 495 US$ 165 12. 10,000 and above US$ 600 US$ 200 Sixth Schedule (Made under regulation 12) 1. Safe Manning Certificate There shall be a charge of US$ 50 which shall be payable for the minimum safe Safe manning survey certificate. 2. Safety Management Certificate There shall be a charge of US$ 500 which shall be payable for issuance of Safety Management Certificate. 3. Document of Compliance (DOC) Certificate There shall be a charge of US$500 which shall be payable for issuance of a Document of Compliance (DOC) Certificate. 4. Surveys for Cargo Ship Safety Construction Certificate There shall be charge for survey for Cargo Ship Safety Construction Certificate, 500 US$ for Ships below 500 Gt; and vessels with and above 500 GT, 500 US$, plus 0.2 US$ per extra GT above 500. 5. Survey for Cargo Ship Safety Equipment Certificate There shall be charges for Cargo Ship Safety Equipment Certificate, 500 US$ for Ships below 500 GT and vessels with and above 500 GT, 500 US$, plus 0.2 US$ per extra GT above 500. 6. Survey for Cargo Ship Safety Radio Certificate There shall be Charge for Survey for Cargo Ship Safety Radio Certificate, 50 US$ for Ships below 500 GT; and Vessels with and above 500 GT, 50 US$, plus 0.2 US$ per extra GT above 500. 7. Inspection of lifesaving appliances and liferaft service stations (1) A charge of USD 30 per surveyor-hour or part thereof shall payable for the examination of the design of a life-saving appliance, for the inspection of the first appliance manufactured to that design and, if required the issuance of a certificate of approval, for the inspection during construction, for there certification or re-certification of a boat or buoyant apparatus, and for the issuance of a report of inspection. (2) The following charges shall payable for the inspection and stamping of life jackets at the manufacturer’s or the Authority’s premises: (a) testing life-jacket - US$ 75 per samples. (b) stamp only: per life-jacket US$ - 0.5; and (c) certificate of approval US$ - 500 (3) A charge of US$ 30 shall be payable per attendance at a life-raft service station at the request of the service station or the master or owner of the ship to which the life raft belongs. (4) A charge of US$50 is payable for the issue of a certificate of approval based on the approval of another competent authority. (5) (a) A charge of US$ 50 is payable for the inspection and approval of a life raft service station and covers two visits by a surveyor. (b) If further visits are required, an additional charge of US$ 30 per surveyor-hour or part thereof is payable. (6) In this paragraph “boat,” means a lifeboat, rescue boat, life raft, survival craft or other boat carried in a ship and intended for use as a life-saving appliance. 8. Inspection of fire appliances service stations (1) A charge of US$ 30 per surveyor-hour or part thereof shall payable for the examination of the design of a fire appliance, for the inspection of the first appliance manufactured to that design and, if required, the issue of a certificate of approval, for the witnessing of tests of fire extinguishers, fire detecting apparatus, smoke helmets and breathing apparatus, fire pumping units, spray nozzles and similar appliances, and for the issue of a report of inspection. (2) A charge of US$ 10 shall payable for the issue of a certificate of approval based on the approval of another competent authority. (3) (a) A charge of US$ 50 shall payable for the inspection and approval of fire appliances service station and covers two visits by a surveyor. (b) If further visits are required, an additional charge of US$ 30 per Surveyor-hour or part thereof shall payable. 9. Inspection of crew accommodation (1) A charge of US$ 3 per compartment shall payable for the issue of a certificate in terms of the Crew Accommodation Regulations (2) A charge of US$ 12 per inspection shall be payable for the inspection of crew accommodation as a result of a complaint about such accommodation, but no charge shall payable if, in the opinion of the officer making the inspection, there were no reasonable grounds for the complaint. 10. Dangerous cargoes The following services charges shall payable for the services referred to, provided in terms of the Merchant Shipping (Dangerous Goods) Regulations:— (a) granting of an exemption - US$ 15 (b) inspection of a ship in which explosives or other dangerous goods are proposed to be carried, to determine— (i) Before loading, whether she ship is equipped and in all other respects suitable to carry such goods - US$ 30 per visit; (ii) Whether the proposed stowage complies with the applicable requirements - US$ 30 per visit; (iii) during loading and upon completion of loading, whether the goods have been handled and Stowed in accordance with the applicable requirements - US$ 30 per visit; (c) Inspection of a magazine or container — the travel and subsistence expenses of the officer concerned and US$ 30 per inspection of not more than five magazines or containers, or, if more than five magazines or containers are to be inspected, US$ 30 plus 30 per surveyor-hour or part thereof; (d) Inspection of a ship for a document of compliance - US$ 55 for two visits to the ship by a surveyor, and for additional visits - US$ 30 per surveyor-hour or part thereof. 11. Grain cargoes The following charges shall payable for the services referred to, provided under the Merchant Shipping (Carriage of Grain in Bulk) Regulations: Item Examination or inspection Charge 1 Initial examination and approval of a grain loading plan for a ship. US$ 120 2. Examination and approval of a grain loading plan for a sister ship-having arrangements similar to those in a ship referred to in item 1. US$ 45 3. Examination and approval of a grain-loading plan that has been altered as a result of alterations to the ship or to the arrangements thereof made after the approval of the US$ 80 4. Inspection on first installation of fittings required by a grain-loading plan, original plan. US$ 35 5. Initial inspection of a ship for a certificate of suitability to load grain, and the final inspection for a grain-loading certificate. US$ 25 for each 1000 tons of cargo or part thereof, subject to a maximum of US$ 160. 12. Timber deck cargoes A charge of US$ 30 per surveyor-hour or part thereof shall be payable for the inspection of the stowage plans, fittings, stowage and securing of timber deck cargoes, subj

definitions.definition: the Merchant Shipping Act, 2003
the Surface and Marine Transport Regulatory Authority
the Code for the Construction and Equipment of Ships Carrying Dangerous Chemicals in Bulk
the total mass of cargo, fuel, fresh water, that a ship can carry when she is floating in salt water with her summer load line at the water surface
an officer whose duties are connected with the deck department
Deadweight
an excavated dock fitted with watertight entrance, from which water can be pumped to allow work to be done on or under the water portion of a docked ship
the International Bulk Carrier Code for the construction and equipment of ships carrying dangerous chemicals in bulk
international code for construction and equipment of ships carrying liquefied gases in bulk
an international pollution prevention certificate for the carriage of Noxious Liquid Substances in bulk issued in conformity with Annex II to MARPOL 73/78
International Oil Pollution Prevention Certificate issued in conformity with annex I to MARPOL 73/78
International Ship & Port Facility Security code
the international convention for the prevention of Pollution from ships, 1973, as modified by the Protocol of 1978 relating thereto
an instrument of security of the kind referred to in section 88 of the Act
any substance referred in appendix II to annex II of MARPOL 73/78
